name: Ransomware - File Encrypted with Suspicious Extension (.safepay / .locked / .encrypted)
description: Detects creation of files with extensions characteristic of ransomware encryption operations. SafePay ransomware appends .safepay after Base64-encoding file contents. Any detection of these extensions on a production endpoint should be treated as a confirmed ransomware incident requiring immediate containment. The rule catches both single-file and mass-encryption scenarios.
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware - Ransom Note File Created (readme_safepay / HOW_TO_DECRYPT)
description: Detects creation of ransom note files in user-accessible directories. Ransom notes are written by ransomware after encryption completes to instruct victims on payment. SafePay drops readme_safepay.txt; other families use HOW_TO_DECRYPT, DECRYPT_INSTRUCTION, or similar names. This is a late-stage, high-confidence indicator that ransomware execution has completed or is near completion.
query:
file where event.action == "creation" and (
  file.name like~ "readme_safepay*" or
  file.name like~ "HOW_TO_DECRYPT*" or
  file.name like~ "DECRYPT_INSTRUCTION*" or
  file.name like~ "YOUR_FILES_ARE_ENCRYPTED*" or
  file.name like~ "RANSOM_NOTE*" or
  file.name like~ "FILES_ENCRYPTED*"
) and (
  file.path like~ "*\\Users\\*" or
  file.path like~ "*\\Desktop\\*" or
  file.path like~ "*\\Documents\\*"
)
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Data Staging - WinRAR Archive Creation with Suspicious Flags
description: Detects WinRAR or rar.exe launched with recursive archiving flags and exfiltration-indicative arguments. Ransomware operators and data-theft extortion groups (including SafePay) use WinRAR to compress and stage stolen data before exfiltration. The rule fires on combinations of the 'a' (add) command, recursive flag, and either multi-threading, high compression, or exfiltration-named output paths. Multiple WinRAR processes spawned within a short window further increases confidence.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=10m
  [process where event.action == "start" and
    process.name in~ ("WinRAR.exe", "rar.exe") and
    process.args : " a " and
    (
      process.command_line like~ "*-r *" or
      process.command_line like~ "*-mt*" or
      process.command_line like~ "*-m3*" or
      process.command_line like~ "*-m5*" or
      process.command_line like~ "*-ep1*" or
      process.command_line like~ "*EXFIL*" or
      process.command_line like~ "*_Archive_*"
    )
  ] with runs=3

technique.name: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility

author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Kill Chain - PowerShell Bypass Leading to Mass File Operations
description: Sequence detection correlating PowerShell execution policy bypass with subsequent mass file creation or deletion in user directories. This multi-stage sequence is characteristic of ransomware that drops and executes a PowerShell orchestration script which then generates, archives, deletes, and encrypts victim files. The sequence must complete on the same host within 5 minutes. SafePay uses powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File to launch its ransomware PowerShell script.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [process where event.action == "start" and
    process.name in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and
    process.command_line like~ "*Bypass*" and
    process.command_line like~ "*-File*"
  ]
  [file where event.action in ("creation", "deletion") and
    (
      file.extension in ("docx", "xlsx", "pdf", "csv", "sql", "bak", "txt")
    ) and
    file.path like~ "*\\Users\\*"
  ] with runs=50
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware - Notepad Displaying Ransom Note (Post-Encryption Indicator)
description: Detects notepad.exe launched with a ransom-note filename in its command line. Ransomware routinely auto-opens the ransom note in Notepad after encryption completes to ensure the victim sees the payment instructions. This is a definitive post-encryption indicator confirming that ransomware has successfully run on the host. SafePay opens readme_safepay.txt via Start-Process notepad.exe.
query:
process where event.action == "start" and
  process.name == "notepad.exe" and
  (
    process.command_line like~ "*readme_safepay*" or
    process.command_line like~ "*HOW_TO_DECRYPT*" or
    process.command_line like~ "*DECRYPT_INSTRUCTION*" or
    process.command_line like~ "*RANSOM*" or
    process.command_line like~ "*YOUR_FILES*"
  )
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
